content:
# 2 John Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2JN_001_page_001.jpg

1. The elder to [the] elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth, and not I only but also all those having known the truth,

2. on account of the truth abiding in us and will be with us forever.

3. Grace, mercy, [and] peace will be with us from God [the] Father and from Yeshua Christos, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

4. I rejoiced greatly that I have found [some] of your children walking in truth, just as we received commandment from the Father.

5. And now I ask you, lady, not as writing a new commandment to you, but [one] which we had from [the] beginning, that we should love one another.

6. And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, just as you heard from [the] beginning, that you should walk in it.

7. Because many deceivers went out into the world, those not confessing Yeshua Christos coming in [the] flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.

8. Watch yourselves, that you do not lose what we worked [for], but that you may receive a full reward.

9. Everyone going ahead and not abiding in the teaching of the Christos does not have God. The [one] abiding in the teaching — this [one] has both the Father and the Son.

10. If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into [your] house and do not say to him, "Greetings,"

11. for the [one] saying to him "Greetings" shares in his evil works.

12. Having many things to write to you, I did not wish [to do so] through paper and ink, but I hope to come to you and speak mouth to mouth, so that our joy may be made full.

13. The children of your elect sister greet you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "The elect lady":** eklektē kyria — debated: is "elect lady" a proper name (Electa, or Kyria), a title for a specific woman, or a metaphor for a church? The latter is most likely, given "her children" and "your elect sister" (v.13).

2. **v.7 — "coming in the flesh":** erchomenon en sarki — present participle: "coming" (not "having come"). The present tense may emphasize the ongoing reality of the incarnation, not just the past event.

3. **v.12 — "mouth to mouth":** stoma pros stoma — literally "mouth to mouth" (not "face to face"). The intimacy of direct speech, not mediated by letter.

— EMET, AI Translator
